org.firstinspires.ftc.teamcode.commandbase.subsystems.swerve

Class OctoSwerveDrivetrainV2

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.firstinspires.ftc.teamcode.commandbase.subsystems.swerve.OctoSwerveDrivetrainV2

  • public class OctoSwerveDrivetrainV2
extends java.lang.Object
    Encoder-agnostic four-module swerve drivetrain controller.

    This is the updated version of OctoSwerveDrivetrain that supports both OctoQuad digital encoders and SRS Hub analog encoders. Switch between encoder types via Constants.SWERVE_ENCODER_TYPE.

    Encoder Support:

    This drivetrain automatically uses the configured encoder type:

    • SwerveEncoderFactory.SwerveEncoderType#OCTOQUAD: High-precision digital encoders with 8192 CPR (recommended)
    • SwerveEncoderFactory.SwerveEncoderType#ANALOG: SRS Hub analog encoders (potentiometers, magnetic encoders)

    How to Switch Encoders:

    Simply change the constant in Constants.java:

     // Use OctoQuad digital encoders (default)
 public static final SwerveEncoderType SWERVE_ENCODER_TYPE = SwerveEncoderType.OCTOQUAD;

 // Use SRS Hub analog encoders
 public static final SwerveEncoderType SWERVE_ENCODER_TYPE = SwerveEncoderType.ANALOG;

    Current Management:

    This drivetrain includes current limiting to prevent 20A fuse trips:

    • Slew Rate Limiting: Prevents rapid power changes (causes current spikes)
    • Current Limiting: Scales power when Floodgage sensor detects high current
    See Also:
    OctoSwerveModuleV2, SwerveEncoder, SwerveEncoderFactory

    • Constructor Detail

      • OctoSwerveDrivetrainV2

        public OctoSwerveDrivetrainV2(double trackWidth,
                              double wheelBase,
                              double maxSpeed,
                              PIDFCoefficients swervoPIDFCoefficients,
                              PIDFCoefficients drivePIDFCoefficients,
                              MotorEx[] motors,
                              CRServoEx[] swervos,
                              OctoQuadFWv3 octoQuad,
                              SRSHub srsHub,
                              double[] moduleOffsets)
        Constructs an encoder-agnostic swerve drivetrain.

        This constructor automatically creates the appropriate encoder type based on Constants.SWERVE_ENCODER_TYPE.

        OctoQuad Port Mapping:

        • Ports 0-3: Steering encoders
        • Ports 4-7: Drive wheel encoders

        SRS Hub Analog Pin Mapping:

        • Pins 1-4: Steering encoders (configured in STEERING_ANALOG_PINS)
        • Drive encoders: Not supported with analog (uses motor encoders)
        Parameters:
        trackWidth - distance between left and right wheels (inches)
        wheelBase - distance between front and back wheels (inches)
        maxSpeed - maximum module velocity (inches/second)
        swervoPIDFCoefficients - PIDF gains for steering
        drivePIDFCoefficients - PIDF gains for drive
        motors - array of 4 drive motors [FR, FL, BL, BR]
        swervos - array of 4 steering servos [FR, FL, BL, BR]
        octoQuad - OctoQuad instance (used if encoder type is OCTOQUAD)
        srsHub - SRS Hub instance (used if encoder type is ANALOG)
        moduleOffsets - array of 4 encoder offsets (radians)
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if arrays are not length 4

    • Method Detail

      • drive

        public void drive(ChassisSpeeds speeds,
                  double currentDraw)
        Commands the drivetrain with current limiting.
        Parameters:
        speeds - chassis velocities to achieve
        currentDraw - current draw from Floodgage sensor (0.0 to disable current limiting)

      • drive

        public void drive(ChassisSpeeds speeds)
        Commands the drivetrain without current limiting.
        Parameters:
        speeds - chassis velocities to achieve

      • drawTelemetry

        public void drawTelemetry(TelemetryPacket packet,
                          Pose robotPose)
        Sends telemetry data to FTC Dashboard.

      • logData

        public void logData(DataLogger logger)
        Logs module state to CSV.

      • stop

        public void stop()
        Stops all modules.

      • isCurrentLimitingActive

        public boolean isCurrentLimitingActive()
        Checks if current limiting is currently reducing motor power.
        Returns:
        true if current limiting is active (scale < 1.0)

      • getCurrentLimitScale

        public double getCurrentLimitScale()
        Gets the current limiting scale factor.
        Returns:
        scale factor (1.0 = no limiting, < 1.0 = limiting active)

      • getEncoderType

        public java.lang.String getEncoderType()
        Gets the current encoder type being used.
        Returns:
        encoder type (e.g., "OctoQuad", "Analog (SRS Hub)")
